data.title: As a developer, resuming a ticket automatically detects and recovers a single abandoned worktree branch
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Adds resume-time scanning logic to sprint and fix-bug that reads WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comments on the top-level ticket and all child story/task tickets, identifies unmatched starts (no corresponding :complete or :landed), and autonomously merges or discards each abandoned branch.

**Why**: Without this logic, interrupted sessions either discard recoverable work silently or duplicate already-completed effort. This story delivers the most common recovery scenario: a single abandoned branch from a prior session.

**Scope**:
- IN: Scanning the top-level epic/bug ticket AND all child story/task tickets (using parent_id filtering via `ticket list`, not `ticket deps`) for unmatched WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comments; for each identified branch — skip if branch no longer exists, treat as already_merged if branch is an ancestor of HEAD (write retroactive :complete, skip re-merge), merge if branch has unique commits and no conflicts, discard if already merged or conflicting; backward-compatibility fallback when no start comment is present (checkpoint-only behavior unchanged); scan covers open and closed tickets; sprint inserts scan in its Auto-Resume Detection section; fix-bug inserts scan after Step 0.5 once the ticket ID is set to in_progress
- OUT: Competing multi-branch tiebreak selection (Story C-Enhancement); the comment write infrastructure (Story A/B)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, at session start sprint and fix-bug scan for WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comments across the top-level ticket and all child story/task tickets (using ticket list + parent_id filtering for two-level enumeration), and for each ticket with an unmatched start, autonomously merge the abandoned branch into the session branch if it has unique commits and no merge conflicts, or discard it if already merged or conflict-prone; if a merge leaves the repo in a mid-merge state due to interruption, `git merge --abort` is called to clean up
  ← Satisfies: "When resuming a ticket with an unmatched WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment (no corresponding complete), the orchestrator autonomously assesses the abandoned branch: merges it into the current session branch if it contains at least one commit not reachable from the session branch HEAD and the merge produces no conflicts; discards it if already merged (no unique commits) or if any merge conflict requires manual resolution."

- When this story is complete, when a WORKTREE_TRACKING:start references a branch that no longer exists, the orchestrator skips it without error and proceeds with the rest of the resume scan
  ← Satisfies: "When a WORKTREE_TRACKING:start references a branch that no longer exists, the orchestrator skips it without error and proceeds."

- When this story is complete, when a branch referenced by a start comment is already an ancestor of HEAD (merged but :complete was never written), the orchestrator treats it as outcome=already_merged, writes a retroactive :complete comment, and does not attempt a re-merge
  ← Satisfies: "When resuming a ticket with an unmatched WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment (no corresponding complete), the orchestrator autonomously assesses the abandoned branch."

- When this story is complete, the resume scan covers both the top-level epic/bug ticket AND all child story/task tickets, identifying any ticket (open or closed) with a start but no corresponding :complete (tasks) or :landed (stories/bugs)
  ← Satisfies: "When resuming, the orchestrator scans for WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comments on both the top-level epic/bug ticket AND all child story and task tickets."

- When this story is complete, when a ticket has no WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment, sprint and fix-bug proceed with the existing checkpoint-only resume behavior unchanged
  ← Satisfies: "When resuming a ticket with no WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment, the orchestrator proceeds with existing checkpoint-only resume behavior unchanged."

- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Implementation] Child ticket enumeration must use `ticket list` + client-side filtering on `parent_id` field — NOT `ticket deps` (which returns dependency relations, not parent-child relations); two-level enumeration required for epic → story → task
- [Implementation] Sprint insertion point: Auto-Resume Detection section (existing SKILL.md structure); fix-bug insertion point: after Step 0.5 after ticket is set to in_progress (fix-bug has no equivalent Auto-Resume Detection section and one must be added)
- [Reliability] If git merge leaves the repository in a mid-merge state (SIGKILL during merge), the orchestrator must call `git merge --abort` to clean up before continuing the resume scan
- [Reliability] Branch already an ancestor of HEAD = outcome=already_merged; do NOT re-merge; write retroactive :complete to close the lifecycle gap
- [Performance] Resume scan iterates over all child tickets — for large epics, minimize ticket CLI calls by reading comment metadata only and skipping tickets with no WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment
- [Scope] debug-everything is a third orchestrator that also resumes bug tickets in Bug-Fix Mode and dispatches sub-agents with worktree isolation; sub-agents dispatched by debug-everything will have :start comments (from Story A) but debug-everything has no resume scan in scope here; document as follow-up work at the docs story or a new bug if the gap is found during implementation

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
